steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Distribute $6x$ across $(x+3)$: multiply $6x$ by each term separately and combine.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$6x^{2}+3$': treats $6x · 3$ as just $3$, dropping the $x$. ✗
  • Choice B — '$6x^{2}+18x$': $6x · x = 6x^2$ and $6x · 3 = 18x$. ✓
  • Choice C — '$7x^{2}+3$': wrong coefficient on $x^2$ and drops the variable from the second term. ✗
  • Choice D — '$7x^{2}+9x$': adds instead of multiplying the leading coefficient, and halves the second term. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
